policy.gt_source: manual inspection of clean_v2 extracted video frames only
policy.notes: Annotated recipe-name cases where the visual query target is consistent with identifying the dish/recipe from the cutting scene.

annotations.visual_reference: recipe scene: slicing fried pork cutlet

expected_region.description: Hands slice an already fried pork cutlet/meat piece into strips on a small cutting board.
expected_region.notes: Frames t12-t14 show the clearest slicing action and the fried meat texture.

detail_gt.evidence: The visual scene shows fried pork/meat being sliced, matching the deep-fried meat platter cooking step.

annotations.visual_reference: recipe corresponding to cutting fried pork chops

expected_region.description: The current action is slicing fried pork/meat into strips.
expected_region.notes: The fried meat and knife action are both clear in this interval.

detail_gt.evidence: The scene shows a fried pork/meat cutlet being cut into strips.

annotations.visual_reference: recipe from database corresponding to current operation

expected_region.description: Knife cuts a browned/deep-fried pork/meat cutlet into parallel strips.
expected_region.notes: This is the clearest portion of the operation.

detail_gt.evidence: The video shows the final cutting step of a fried meat/pork dish.

annotations.visual_reference: recipe being prepared from the current cutting scene

expected_region.description: A deep-fried pork/meat cutlet is sliced on the cutting board.
expected_region.notes: Frames t12-t14 show the object and action clearly.

detail_gt.evidence: The visible dish is a deep-fried pork/meat cutlet being cut into strips.

left_pending_by_policy.reason: The source instruction visually asks for the dish/recipe, but the extracted visual query target is ingredient_name. These need schema review before GT should be written.
These ask for home inventory/storage/category/business facts after recipe resolution, not a directly visible referent in the video.
